title: [WIP] Use Version from spec file in setup.py
body:
- If envvar PODMAN_VERSION not set use default version of 0.0.0

Signed-off-by: Jhon Honce <jhonce@redhat.com>

comments.body: @lsm5 When we were talking about automating the setup.py version number this is what I was trying to convey.
I guess setup.py can not take arguments to set the version.
@rhatdan Correct.

Clever.
LGTM
@rh-atomic-bot r+

:pushpin: Commit 4654ba3 has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan 4654ba31952c27a879c3c6724c8c145e2c7c930a -->
:hourglass: Testing commit 4654ba3 with merge 727ecfe...
@jwhonce Got carried away and just merged this.  Any reason not to?

> Got carried away and just merged this. Any reason not to?

The `setup.py` change is is going to get clobbered by the release script from #867.  If we want to keep this PR, we'll need to update that script to avoid touching `setup.py`.

@rhatdan @jwhonce sorry, just seeing this now, as far as the official rpm goes, we'll need to get the version somewhere from the source, so setup.py or spec.in, doesn't make a difference ....also, didn't we decide to use the same version number for both podman and python3-podman? Right now the canonical podman.spec mentions just a single version for the entire package.

     * Fri May 18 2018 Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@fedoraproject.org> - 0.5.3-7.gitc54b423
     - make python3-podman the same version as the main package
     - build python3-podman only for fedora >= 28

> ... also, didn't we decide to use the same version number for both podman and python3-podman?

That's the way I went with the #867 release script, so if that gets used for today's release `setup.py` will end up with 0.6.1 there.
Yup I screwed up in appoving this. Not sure if there is a way to stop it. homu is not supposed to merge WIP projects, if it checks at the end.

:sunny: Test successful - [status-papr](https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/libpod/727ecfeadf09edef520513513be78d7d93221a3b.1527885548234700800/index.html)
Approved by: rhatdan
Pushing 727ecfeadf09edef520513513be78d7d93221a3b to master...
But with this patch we should still get the podman version from the spec file.
@rhatdan no worries as far as the rpms go though
Are we leaving this in?  If so, should I file a PR to update the release script?
@wking please do, thanks.
@wking Yes, I don't see an issue with @jwhonce patch.  So we need to just fix the script to do nothing with python and we should be all set.

@rhatdan @jwhonce just so we're on the same page, as far as rpms go, those are using the same Version for podman and python3-podman and RE: rawhide autobuilds, those will just grab the version from `version/version.go`. In rpms, we're not using the version from `contrib/spec/podman.spec.in`.

If we're going back to separate versions, I'll need to do Epoch hocus-pocus for python3-podman since podman version is already ahead of python3-podman. I'll vote for splitting it out into a separate repo in that case.
@lsm5 The rpm's version # doesn't come from the spec file?

> ... those will just grab the version from `version/version.go`. In rpms, we're not using the version from `contrib/spec/podman.spec.in`.

With #879, the release script will [make sure those are in sync for releases][1].

[1]: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/879/commits/06d3c96a3c2fe0e9f845adc370601d2886f4be83#diff-cede583a27c227d458ca67079957c8a2R47

> The rpm's version # doesn't come from the spec file?

@jwhonce the fedora rpm doesn't depend in any way on `contrib/spec/podman.spec.in` if I understood your question correctly.

@jwhonce as far as grabbing version numbers for the official rpm goes, it doesn't make a difference whether it's from setup.py or contrib/spec/podman.spec.in, but just that having 2 separate version numbers in the same package can be painful for RHEL and non-rawhide builds, that's why my ask for separate repo.
Yes we want them to be the same version until we split the two packages up.
